evals.name: forge-cold-start
evals.prompt: I'm working in a fresh Node.js + Express + TypeScript project (src/, tests/, package.json present). Create a Spec Mint spec for adding token-bucket rate limiting to the /api/auth/login endpoint. Use the full forge workflow.
evals.expected_output: Three files in .specs/<spec-id>/: SPEC.html, research-01.md, interview-01.md. Registry.md updated. SPEC.html is valid HTML with single-line spec-meta JSON in canonical key order, every region paired (opener/endregion), at least one Mermaid diagram with quoted aliases/labels, code-diff previews, no ASCII art. Validator (`specmintValidate()`) reports 0 errors.
expectations: `.specs/<spec-id>/SPEC.html` exists and the file content begins with `<!doctype html>` (case-insensitive)
`.specs/<spec-id>/research-01.md` exists and is non-empty (>500 characters of actual research notes, not a stub)
`.specs/<spec-id>/interview-01.md` exists and contains at least one user-answered question
`.specs/<spec-id>/SPEC.html` contains a `<script type="application/json" id="spec-meta">` element whose JSON parses
spec-meta JSON keys appear in canonical order: id, title, status, created, updated, priority, tags, mockup-fidelity
Every `<!-- region:NAME -->` in SPEC.html has a matching `<!-- endregion:NAME -->`
At least one `<pre class="mermaid">` block exists inside a `<figure class="diagram">`
Every participant in any sequenceDiagram has its alias wrapped in double quotes (zero unquoted `participant X as something(with parens)` lines)
`.specs/assets/spec-styles.css` and `.specs/assets/spec-runtime.js` exist (copied from plugin assets/)
Registry.md contains a row for the new spec with status=active

evals.name: resume-existing-spec
evals.prompt: Resume the active spec in this project.
evals.expected_output: AI reads .specs/registry.md, identifies the active spec, opens its SPEC.html, finds the first `<li class="task" data-status="pending">` in the in-progress phase, and announces it without modifying any state. Does NOT swap data-status or write to disk.
expectations: AI's response identifies the spec's title and ID from the registry
AI's response names the specific task code (e.g., [AUTH-06]) of the first pending task
AI reports the current phase by name (not just number)
No `data-status` attribute in SPEC.html changed from before to after the response
No new files written under `.specs/<id>/`

evals.name: pause-and-switch
evals.prompt: Pause the active spec and switch to the spec called 'api-refactor'. Report what changed.
evals.expected_output: Both the current spec's status and registry row become 'paused'. The api-refactor spec's status and row become 'active'. Pause happens at a task boundary (no half-edits). Decision Log of the paused spec gets a 'Session paused on <date>' entry if non-trivial work happened.
expectations: The previously active spec's `<script id="spec-meta">` shows `"status":"paused"` after the action
The api-refactor spec's `<script id="spec-meta">` shows `"status":"active"` after the action
`.specs/registry.md` has exactly one row with `active` status after the action
The validate recipe (`python3 -c ...`) returns exit code 0 on both SPEC.html files after the swap
AI reports both transitions in a single response

evals.name: mermaid-always-quote
evals.prompt: In the Architecture section of this SPEC.html, add a sequenceDiagram showing a request through Frontend (React, /sites) -> Backend API (port 8080) -> PostgreSQL, with arrows labeled 'POST /api/sites (body)' and 'INSERT INTO sites'. Then run specmintValidate() and confirm zero parse errors.
evals.expected_output: The new <pre class="mermaid"> renders without errors. Every participant alias is in double quotes (including 'Frontend (React, /sites)' which has parens AND a comma — exactly the pattern that has broken parses before). Every message label is in double quotes. specmintValidate() reports zero [mermaid] errors.
expectations: The new sequenceDiagram block contains exactly 3 `participant` lines
Every `participant` line matches the regex `participant \S+ as "[^"]+"` (alias wrapped in double quotes)
Every arrow line matches `->>` or `-->>` followed by `: "..."` (label wrapped in double quotes)
Running `mermaid.parse(src)` on the new block source resolves without throwing
After running `specmintValidate()` in the page console, the returned array contains zero objects with `area === 'mermaid'`

evals.name: code-preview-density
evals.prompt: Forge a spec for a 5-phase migration from REST to GraphQL across an existing API + frontend (3 modules touched). Include the right number of code-diff previews per the SKILL.md sizing guidance.
evals.expected_output: Per the new sizing rule (medium spec = 5-10 previews), the spec should ship at least 5 and at most 12 `<figure class="code-diff">` blocks. Categories represented: new public interface/contract, new data model/schema, non-trivial business logic, before→after of a significant refactor. No boilerplate previews (imports-only diffs, route-registration-only).
expectations: SPEC.html contains between 5 and 12 `<figure class="code-diff">` elements
At least one code-diff has `data-language` set to a non-empty value
No code-diff figure contains ONLY import/require statement changes (boilerplate filter)
At least one code-diff shows a before→after of a function signature or type change

evals.name: mockup-no-ascii
evals.prompt: Forge a spec for an admin dashboard that lists pending invites, with `mockup-fidelity: wireframe`. Include at least one mockup of the dashboard.
evals.expected_output: The UI Mockups section contains one or more `<figure class="mockup mockup--wireframe">` blocks composed from `.wf-*` primitives (`.wf-frame`, `.wf-row`, `.wf-table`, `.wf-card`, etc.). Zero ASCII-art boxes drawn with `+`, `-`, `|`. Zero `<pre>` elements inside any `figure.mockup`.
expectations: SPEC.html contains at least one `<figure class="mockup">` element
Each mockup figure contains at least one `<div class="wf-frame">` or equivalent .wf-* primitive (not just text)
Zero `<pre>` elements appear inside `<figure class="mockup">` (the runtime validator's mockup check returns 0 hits)
Combined occurrences of `+--` and `|--` substrings inside any `figure.mockup` text is 0
